THOUGHT:
Reviewing the current citation list, I have covered Categories 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, and 7. The most critical missing categories are 6 (Using Established Methods) and 8 (Suggesting Future Research). For this round, I will prioritize Category 6, as a rigorous discussion of real-world DL pitfalls must ground its analysis in established methodological baselines. Specifically, I need a foundational paper on neural network calibration and uncertainty estimation, since model overconfidence on distributionally shifted or out-of-distribution data is one of the most pervasive and dangerous real-world failure modes. This paper will serve as the methodological anchor for evaluating trustworthiness and will directly support our experimental framework. I will reserve Category 8 for the final round.
